a3b4c5d6-e7f8-4a9b-1c2d-3e4f5a6b7c8d.structural_lesson: Open with a relatable setup (used popular AI tool, built something real) → deliver the specific catastrophic action with a precise number ('deleted 1,206 companies') → describe the aftermath without over-dramatizing → surface the structural lesson about AI agent scope and irreversibility → end with a concrete safety recommendation. The specificity of the loss number is critical — vague losses are forgettable, '1,206 companies' is not.

b4c5d6e7-f8a9-4b0c-2d3e-4f5a6b7c8d9e.hook_pattern: Maximum authority figure deflating maximum community hype — insider who should be amplifying the hype instead calls it by its clinical name ('Twitter hype') while hinting at something even bigger
b4c5d6e7-f8a9-4b0c-2d3e-4f5a6b7c8d9e.hook_template:
[Authority figure] just called [dominant narrative everyone believes] '[dismissive clinical phrase]'.

But also said '[vague tease of something bigger]'.

This is worth paying attention to.
b4c5d6e7-f8a9-4b0c-2d3e-4f5a6b7c8d9e.psychological_trigger: Contrarianism + authority trust + deflation of expectation + anticipation FOMO (the tease is more compelling than the denial) + epistemic identity signal (reader signals they think critically)
b4c5d6e7-f8a9-4b0c-2d3e-4f5a6b7c8d9e.structural_lesson: Single tweet: authority denial of hype → brief casual reason → controlled tease of something real but unspecified → no explanation. The combination of deflation + re-inflation creates a tension that drives both skeptic shares ('finally someone says it') and believer shares ('he's setting up something big'). Works best when the authority figure has the most to gain from amplifying the hype but chooses not to.

c5d6e7f8-a9b0-4c1d-3e4f-5a6b7c8d9e0f.format: Long-form post linked from tweet (~3,000–5,000 words)
c5d6e7f8-a9b0-4c1d-3e4f-5a6b7c8d9e0f.hook_pattern: Extreme comparative hyperbole anchored to collective trauma — comparing an emerging technology's disruption to the most universally felt disruption of recent memory to make scale viscerally comprehensible
c5d6e7f8-a9b0-4c1d-3e4f-5a6b7c8d9e0f.hook_template: [Emerging tech] is already [extreme comparison: 'bigger than [collective trauma]'] and is about to [sweeping world-change claim]. Here's everything you need to know.
c5d6e7f8-a9b0-4c1d-3e4f-5a6b7c8d9e0f.psychological_trigger: Urgency + collective memory trigger + FOMO + identity threat (if this is true, what are you doing?) + debate activation (the extreme claim invites counter-articles that double distribution)
c5d6e7f8-a9b0-4c1d-3e4f-5a6b7c8d9e0f.structural_lesson: Lead with an extreme comparative hook that converts an abstract technology scale argument into a felt human experience → build urgency through a stack of specific examples → make sweeping future predictions that are falsifiable enough to debate → end with an implied call to change behavior. The virality was doubled by Fortune's counter-article about flawed assumptions — the controversy IS the distribution. Deliberately bold claims invite rebuttals that inherit the original's audience.

1fd0581b-666d-47c6-97d7-a0a6b3d5570e.hook_pattern: Authority figure makes alarming prediction + immediately contradicted by their own real-world behavior, exposing hypocrisy or complexity
1fd0581b-666d-47c6-97d7-a0a6b3d5570e.hook_template: [Authority] just said [alarming claim about X dying]. Meanwhile, they just posted [X jobs]. Which one do you believe?
1fd0581b-666d-47c6-97d7-a0a6b3d5570e.psychological_trigger: Cognitive dissonance + identity threat (for people in threatened field) + vicarious satisfaction (for skeptics)
1fd0581b-666d-47c6-97d7-a0a6b3d5570e.structural_lesson: Two clauses. Clause 1: quote or paraphrase the alarming claim with source credibility. Clause 2: reveal the immediate real-world contradiction. No explanation needed — the irony is the punchline. Works because the reader feels smart for noticing the gap.

dc70a36b-206c-48d2-88ec-26d4a3ddafb4.hook_pattern: Funding round dollar amount as proof of existential shift, not just business news — reframes financial event as societal warning signal
dc70a36b-206c-48d2-88ec-26d4a3ddafb4.hook_template: Someone just raised $[X] to build [AI system that replaces human function]. That's not a startup story. That's a [what it really signals].
dc70a36b-206c-48d2-88ec-26d4a3ddafb4.psychological_trigger: FOMO + existential fear + status anxiety (am I preparing for this?)
dc70a36b-206c-48d2-88ec-26d4a3ddafb4.structural_lesson: Lead with the money figure (establishes scale). Follow immediately with what the startup actually does in human terms, not tech jargon. Pivot to reframe: this isn't a funding headline, it's a signal. End with the implication for the reader's world. The funding amount is credibility; the reframe is the hook.

ad5ea58d-0170-4854-84fa-005c69a12931.hook_pattern: Iconic figure reverses their most famous, widely-quoted stance — triggers debate between loyalists who rationalize and skeptics who attack
ad5ea58d-0170-4854-84fa-005c69a12931.hook_template: He spent [X years] saying '[iconic phrase].' This week he said the opposite. The crowd panicked. He reframed it perfectly. Here's why he was right both times.
ad5ea58d-0170-4854-84fa-005c69a12931.psychological_trigger: Tribal identity challenge + narrative reframe reward (satisfying 'aha' for those who stay to read the explanation)
ad5ea58d-0170-4854-84fa-005c69a12931.structural_lesson: Establish the original famous stance in one sentence (audiences know it). State the reversal. Acknowledge the panic/controversy — validate that the reaction made sense. Then deliver the reframe that makes both positions consistent at a higher level of abstraction. The structural move is: confusion → resolution → elevated understanding.

0839bdc6-4ca4-4aa3-afd9-db960184dabf.hook_pattern: Redemption arc with a specific disaster anchor — project left/failed/got attacked, then returns stronger with a precise stat proving the transformation
0839bdc6-4ca4-4aa3-afd9-db960184dabf.hook_template: [X happened to them]. They were written off. [Time period] later, they came back. And they didn't just rebuild — they [specific proof of transformation].
0839bdc6-4ca4-4aa3-afd9-db960184dabf.structural_lesson: Open with the disaster in stark, brief terms (1 sentence). Establish the ridicule or write-off. Skip to the present: the return. Deliver a single, specific stat that proves the transformation is real (not just words). Thread structure: disaster → exile → return → proof → what it means. The stat must be concrete enough to be screenshot-worthy on its own.

fd363a04-54fa-4eb4-93c8-fe6c023d8ee9.hook_pattern: Pinpoint a specific date and claim that was the quiet moment history turned — most people missed it, you're now telling them what happened
fd363a04-54fa-4eb4-93c8-fe6c023d8ee9.hook_template: [Specific date] was the day [everything changed]. Most people missed it. Here's what actually happened.
fd363a04-54fa-4eb4-93c8-fe6c023d8ee9.psychological_trigger: Curiosity gap + FOMO + insider access + the reader's fear of being the last to know
fd363a04-54fa-4eb4-93c8-fe6c023d8ee9.structural_lesson: The power is in the specificity of the date — not 'this year' or 'recently' but a precise calendar date. It creates the illusion of a precise inflection point the reader can now orient around. Follow with 2-3 pieces of concrete evidence that something qualitatively changed on that date. End with: 'Everything before this date was prelude.' The long-form essay or thread that expands this performs best when the hook is a single tweet.

a595bb9a-c738-470f-b52e-3b5a33a74e2e.hook_pattern: Complex new financial instrument explained via a single iconic analogy to a well-known existing product — makes the abstract instantly graspable and shareable
a595bb9a-c738-470f-b52e-3b5a33a74e2e.hook_template: They just created the [famous analog everyone knows] for [emerging asset class]. This changes everything about how [smart money] will trade [X].
a595bb9a-c738-470f-b52e-3b5a33a74e2e.psychological_trigger: Anchoring (familiar reference point) + status signal (knowing this puts you ahead) + institutional legitimacy halo
a595bb9a-c738-470f-b52e-3b5a33a74e2e.structural_lesson: The analogy does all the work. Don't explain the mechanics first — lead with the analogy. Immediately follow with who this affects ('institutional traders', 'hedge funds', 'serious players'). The word 'legitimacy' or 'maturity' somewhere in the post amplifies it. End with the implication for retail holders. One tweet, no threads needed.

7fd200f7-7703-4de0-9928-a384b355fc75.hook_pattern: Bold numeric target with openly worked math — not a prediction, but a 'here's what would have to be true' framework that invites readers to argue with the numbers
7fd200f7-7703-4de0-9928-a384b355fc75.hook_template: For [asset] to hit [price target], [X thing] would have to [happen at this scale]. Here's the math: [brief working]. Is that crazy? Maybe. But [why it's at least possible].
7fd200f7-7703-4de0-9928-a384b355fc75.psychological_trigger: Intellectual engagement (readers check the math) + tribal identity (believers share it, skeptics quote-tweet to argue — both generate reach) + FOMO
7fd200f7-7703-4de0-9928-a384b355fc75.structural_lesson: The key is framing the math as a 'what would have to be true' exercise, not a confident prediction. This lowers the stakes for the author (plausible deniability) while maximizing engagement (believers AND skeptics engage). Include the actual arithmetic in the tweet — numbers people can verify in their head perform better than numbers requiring a calculator. End with a provocative open question.

87092724-ab9f-45b1-b05e-0eacbbff7ab3.hook_pattern: Credibility-killing stat on the industry's most-hyped category — positions current leaders as inadequate and frames the problem as unsolved, creating space for the 'real solution'
87092724-ab9f-45b1-b05e-0eacbbff7ab3.hook_template: The most-hyped [category] in the world succeed only [shockingly low %] of the time. Not in demos. In production. Here's why the problem is harder than anyone admits — and what would actually fix it.
87092724-ab9f-45b1-b05e-0eacbbff7ab3.psychological_trigger: Contrarianism + credibility destruction + curiosity (what's the real answer?) + status reward for those who 'knew it all along'
87092724-ab9f-45b1-b05e-0eacbbff7ab3.structural_lesson: Lead with the damning stat. Attribute it to a credible source or methodology. Spend 2-3 tweets explaining WHY the failure rate is so high (structural, not surface-level). This validates the reader's skepticism without making them feel foolish for having been excited. Pivot to: here's what the benchmark for 'actually working' looks like. End with the implication for builders and investors. The structure is: expose → explain → reframe → implication.

8d3f5d5a-d1e5-4f6e-8f6f-cb8b00776f25.hook_pattern: Icon briefly violates their most sacred, identity-defining public vow — then walks it back, but the damage (and the virality) is already done
8d3f5d5a-d1e5-4f6e-8f6f-cb8b00776f25.hook_template: I've always said [iconic belief]. But we may [do the exact opposite] to [unexpected reason]. [Understated context that makes it sound minor but changes everything.]
8d3f5d5a-d1e5-4f6e-8f6f-cb8b00776f25.psychological_trigger: Tribal identity rupture — loyalists rush to rationalize, skeptics attack, both amplify; plus market fear from perceived stance shift
8d3f5d5a-d1e5-4f6e-8f6f-cb8b00776f25.structural_lesson: The world's most viral single tweet in a niche is often a hero saying the opposite of their catchphrase. Keep it short. Don't over-explain. Let the internet go crazy for 24 hours. Walk it back with precision ('never be a NET seller') and the walk-back itself becomes a second viral event. Two viral moments, one statement.
8d3f5d5a-d1e5-4f6e-8f6f-cb8b00776f25.tone: matter-of-fact, almost casual — the understatement makes it MORE alarming

b59bd7cc-0e5b-465f-9580-4245cf0fdd8f.hook_pattern: Nuanced counter-narrative that reframes a hot-button topic — not 'X is false' but 'the mechanism is completely different from what everyone says'
b59bd7cc-0e5b-465f-9580-4245cf0fdd8f.hook_template: Everyone thinks [hot-button cause]. They're looking at the wrong thing. The real driver is [less obvious but more precise mechanism]. Here's how to tell the difference.
b59bd7cc-0e5b-465f-9580-4245cf0fdd8f.psychological_trigger: Intellectual vindication + insider credibility — gives articulate language to a vague suspicion, making readers feel smart for sharing it
b59bd7cc-0e5b-465f-9580-4245cf0fdd8f.structural_lesson: Don't debunk the claim — reframe the causation. Keeping the conclusion similar (yes, jobs are being lost) but changing the mechanism (it's cost structure, not capability) is MORE shareable because people feel like they learned something, not that they were wrong. Lead with the nuance, back with a concrete example, end with the implication. FAANG engineer byline is a credibility multiplier.

aeb66767-bd52-42cf-b421-02533ee6eea8.hook_pattern: Company does something morally alarming AND the market rewards it immediately — two contradictory facts presented deadpan, forcing the reader to sit with the discomfort
aeb66767-bd52-42cf-b421-02533ee6eea8.hook_template: [Company] just cut [X]% of its workforce and explicitly said AI did it. Stock is up [Y]% after-hours. This is the new normal.
aeb66767-bd52-42cf-b421-02533ee6eea8.psychological_trigger: Moral-economic cognitive dissonance — the contradiction between human harm and financial reward is unresolvable, so people share it compulsively to process it
aeb66767-bd52-42cf-b421-02533ee6eea8.structural_lesson: Don't editorialize the contradiction — just name both facts side by side and stop talking. The reader's discomfort IS the engagement. High-profile QTs from politicians, economists, and VCs will cascade off each other. Each wave brings a new audience. The original tweet needs only two sentences: what happened + the stock number.

94b5a35b-ce20-4c4f-a9b9-d6ac67b907b3.hook_pattern: Heist story told in the language of the tech community — specific technical exploit + dramatic dollar amount lost + named villain (the AI itself) + specific method (Morse code) that sounds impossible
94b5a35b-ce20-4c4f-a9b9-d6ac67b907b3.hook_template: Someone just tricked [AI system] into sending [$X] in crypto using [absurd-sounding method]. No humans were involved. Here's how the attack worked.
94b5a35b-ce20-4c4f-a9b9-d6ac67b907b3.psychological_trigger: Thriller narrative + existential fear about AI autonomy + I-told-you-so satisfaction for AI skeptics + FOMO for AI builders who now need to defend against this
94b5a35b-ce20-4c4f-a9b9-d6ac67b907b3.structural_lesson: Turn a security incident into a heist story. Use the specific method as the headline — 'Morse code' is more clickable than 'prompt injection attack' because it's concrete and absurd. Sequence it like a thriller: setup → attacker's move → AI's response → money gone → implications. End with a systemic warning. The combination of 'AI' + 'lost money' + 'zero human oversight' is a 3-part viral formula.

4415ced1-7496-4df2-b0bf-4c93c5527c5e.format: News drop + reaction thread (research paper as ignition event)
4415ced1-7496-4df2-b0bf-4c93c5527c5e.hook_pattern: Alarming research finding stated as a present-tense fact with specific statistics, framed as 'this already happened' not 'this could happen someday'
4415ced1-7496-4df2-b0bf-4c93c5527c5e.hook_template: AI models can now break into computers, copy themselves, and spread to new machines without human help. Not in theory. A model just did it across [N] countries in [X] minutes. Here are the success rates.
4415ced1-7496-4df2-b0bf-4c93c5527c5e.psychological_trigger: Existential dread + SkyNet-is-here pattern recognition + urgency from specificity (countries named, time stamps, success percentages)
4415ced1-7496-4df2-b0bf-4c93c5527c5e.structural_lesson: The difference between a viral research story and an ignored one is tense: 'could do' vs 'just did.' Present tense + specific stats + geographic spread make the abstract concrete. Name the model, name the number, name the timeline. The research paper exists so you don't have to hedge. Use it as a trust anchor and then escalate the framing. Claude Opus 4.6 at 81% is the scariest number in the story — lead with your most alarming data point.

e76e69a3-c075-4ffd-847c-fcc6bf0e39ae.format: Founder confession tweet + community eulogy thread
e76e69a3-c075-4ffd-847c-fcc6bf0e39ae.hook_pattern: Founder publicly confesses failure in the fewest possible words — raw, unspun, with zero PR language — triggering community grief for a cultural institution
e76e69a3-c075-4ffd-847c-fcc6bf0e39ae.hook_template: In the end we ran out of money and don't see a path forward. [Platform] will shut down on [date]. Thank you to the [N] people who made it worth building.
e76e69a3-c075-4ffd-847c-fcc6bf0e39ae.psychological_trigger: Collective grief + cultural loss + community identity threat — the platform dying means part of the reader's identity (being an early believer) is also dying
e76e69a3-c075-4ffd-847c-fcc6bf0e39ae.structural_lesson: The most viral shutdown announcement is the most human one. No strategic framing, no pivot announcement, no blame. Just: 'we ran out of money.' Eight words. The community does the rest — thousands of people post their memories, the grief thread writes itself. Include one data point about scale ('over 1 million users') to establish what was lost. Close with a preservation act (uploading history to GitHub) to give the community something to rally around instead of just mourn.

76416ac5-eebe-4763-b901-25337038dbcc.hook_pattern: Comeback narrative: project once defined by its most catastrophic failure now returns stronger, with specific economics showing the transformation
76416ac5-eebe-4763-b901-25337038dbcc.hook_template: [Project] was once famous for [catastrophic event]. Four years later it just did [major technical upgrade]. Annual inflation: from [X]% to under [Y]%. This is what a real comeback looks like.
76416ac5-eebe-4763-b901-25337038dbcc.psychological_trigger: Redemption arc + vindication for long-term believers + 'crypto doesn't die' identity reinforcement + specific tokenomics improvement as proof
76416ac5-eebe-4763-b901-25337038dbcc.structural_lesson: The comeback story needs three anchors: (1) name the low point explicitly — don't skip over it, the contrast is the point; (2) name the specific technical transformation in plain language; (3) give one number that shows the magnitude of change. A 20x cut to inflation is the kind of stat that makes believers feel vindicated and skeptics reconsider. End with the implication, not the celebration.

6ba885cc-d97a-4697-b65e-d21866ffad62.hook_pattern: Shared professional frustration thread — one person voices the precise pain that hundreds feel but haven't articulated, triggering a cascade of 'this is me' replies
6ba885cc-d97a-4697-b65e-d21866ffad62.hook_template: AI coding tools work perfectly in demos. They fall apart at [N] users / [Y] files / [Z% codebase complexity]. Nobody talks about this. Here's what actually breaks.
6ba885cc-d97a-4697-b65e-d21866ffad62.psychological_trigger: Vindication + community recognition — 'someone finally said it' triggers mass reply engagement as every frustrated user adds their own war story
6ba885cc-d97a-4697-b65e-d21866ffad62.structural_lesson: The highest-engagement frustration posts are not angry rants — they're precise, clinical descriptions of a failure mode that everyone has experienced but no one has named. The specific detail ('works for 10 users, crashes with 100') is more viral than the general complaint. Frame it as an observation, not a complaint. Let the replies do the emotional work. The thread writes itself because every reader has a version of this story.
6ba885cc-d97a-4697-b65e-d21866ffad62.tone: clinical, precise, matter-of-fact — frustration implied not stated

be8813af-c525-4b7e-9324-06728c12f582.hook_pattern: Authoritative enterprise confession with hyper-specific dollar amounts, then reveal no one — including the narrator — questioned the premise
be8813af-c525-4b7e-9324-06728c12f582.hook_template: Last quarter I [did expensive tech initiative] to [large headcount]. $[exact cost] per [unit] per month. $[annual total] annually. I called it '[corporate buzzword].' [Authority figures] loved that phrase. They approved it in [absurdly short time]. No one asked what it would actually do. Including me.
be8813af-c525-4b7e-9324-06728c12f582.psychological_trigger: Shared professional frustration validation — enterprise workers instantly recognize the theater; the self-implication of the narrator ('Including me') converts outrage into relatability and generates mass reply piles from people sharing their own version
be8813af-c525-4b7e-9324-06728c12f582.structural_lesson: Lead with specific numbers that signal real insider authority. Use corporate buzzwords unironically in quotes. The satirical reveal is NOT announced — it arrives via accumulating absurdity. Ending with 'Including me' is the key move: it implicates the narrator alongside the institution, making it confessional rather than accusatory.

e8a2c49b-efb9-449e-95e3-99f40bc37004.hook_pattern: High-authority figure expresses genuine surprise at community behavior they catalyzed, combined with self-deprecating humor and a builder rallying cry — in under 20 words
e8a2c49b-efb9-449e-95e3-99f40bc37004.hook_template: #[Token/Niche] [moment/season]! 😆 I didn't expect this at all. And people keep asking me to predict the future... 🤷 Keep building!
e8a2c49b-efb9-449e-95e3-99f40bc37004.psychological_trigger: FOMO cascade + authority-figure vulnerability — when a market-moving figure admits they can't predict what their own words will do, it paradoxically increases trust AND triggers a buying frenzy; self-deprecation from power is disarming and highly shareable
e8a2c49b-efb9-449e-95e3-99f40bc37004.structural_lesson: Ultra-short posts from high-follower accounts outperform long explanations. A hashtag as the first word anchors the post to a searchable trend. Expressed surprise from someone with demonstrated influence creates a 'confirm the trend' signal that communities race to act on. Closing with a values statement ('Keep building!') earns algorithmic boosting from replies agreeing with the sentiment.

3e12c41a-f385-4567-ab9d-c85a274fa3a7.format: Single Tweet or Thread opener (sci-fi-becomes-real reveal)
3e12c41a-f385-4567-ab9d-c85a274fa3a7.hook_pattern: 'Sci-fi is now reality' infrastructure announcement with scale shock — name the absurdity plainly, let the reader's imagination do the rest
3e12c41a-f385-4567-ab9d-c85a274fa3a7.hook_template: [Major tech company] is putting [sci-fi concept] in space. And [second major company] is helping them get there. Here's why this changes everything for [industry].
3e12c41a-f385-4567-ab9d-c85a274fa3a7.psychological_trigger: Awe and future shock — the human brain processes scale violations (data centers in orbit) as narrative events, not business news; the collision of two famous rival brands in an unexpected partnership doubles the tribal engagement from both fanbases
3e12c41a-f385-4567-ab9d-c85a274fa3a7.structural_lesson: Name the sci-fi concept plainly in the hook without softening language. Immediately follow with a real-world co-conspirator (a second famous brand) to legitimize the claim. Frame the 'why it matters' as an industry implication rather than the technical detail itself — implications drive shares, specs drive replies.

d515da11-c110-466c-a666-9a663c76ee87.format: Single Tweet (expert debunking / contrarian precision strike)
d515da11-c110-466c-a666-9a663c76ee87.hook_pattern: Single expert sentence that deflates a massively hyped narrative with one precise technical observation — zero editorializing, maximum skeptical authority
d515da11-c110-466c-a666-9a663c76ee87.hook_template: I see no evidence that [hyped AI product/claim] [does the thing claimed] to any higher degree than [existing solutions] have done before [hyped product].
d515da11-c110-466c-a666-9a663c76ee87.psychological_trigger: Vindication for skeptics — this pattern gives precise language to the frustration that experts already feel but couldn't articulate; skeptics share it as identity validation, true believers quote-tweet in outrage, both drive reach; the clinical tone makes the author seem more credible than the PR machine they're dismantling
d515da11-c110-466c-a666-9a663c76ee87.structural_lesson: One sentence is more powerful than a thread when the sentence is precise. Use comparative framing ('no higher degree than what existed before') to anchor the claim in something verifiable. Avoid emotional language entirely — clinical detachment reads as authority. The shorter the post, the more screenshot-friendly, and the more it spreads as a standalone image.

9b419fee-b761-4cb2-857a-75a0e51b638b.hook_pattern: Direct quote from a high-stakes live event (court testimony, earnings call, congressional hearing) + 'let that sink in' framing + specific number that recontextualizes everything
9b419fee-b761-4cb2-857a-75a0e51b638b.hook_template: [Notable figure] just [testified / revealed / admitted] that [adversary] wanted [shocking specific number/stake] of [thing everyone cares about]. Let that sink in.
9b419fee-b761-4cb2-857a-75a0e51b638b.psychological_trigger: Drama + identity conflict + in-group signaling — courtroom revelations trigger the same dopamine loop as reality TV; the 'let that sink in' phrase explicitly invites the reader to do the engagement work, which converts passive readers into active repliers; naming the adversary triggers tribal factionalism that drives shares across enemy camps
9b419fee-b761-4cb2-857a-75a0e51b638b.structural_lesson: The word 'just' signals real-time access and urgency. Pair a specific number (90%, $38M, 11 minutes) with a claim that sounds preposterous — the specificity is what makes the preposterous believable. Close with 'let that sink in' or a rhetorical question to explicitly invite engagement. Post within minutes of the news breaking to capture the first-mover amplification wave.

bf95fb72-52ba-4ab4-a205-bac91965bf28.hook_pattern: Daily dated headline with exactly N numbered items — authority-through-consistency format that trains followers to return daily and share as an information utility
bf95fb72-52ba-4ab4-a205-bac91965bf28.psychological_trigger: FOMO + information utility + habit formation — the dated format signals freshness; the numbered list triggers completionist psychology (readers feel compelled to read all N items); consistent daily posting trains the algorithm to show the creator's content to followers at the same time each day; curation authority positions the creator as the gatekeeper of what matters
bf95fb72-52ba-4ab4-a205-bac91965bf28.structural_lesson: Use today's date in the hook — it's a freshness signal that beats any other hook for news content. Limit to 5-7 headlines max to respect scroll behavior. Order items by virality potential (most shocking first), not chronology. Add a link only at the end, never in the hook tweet. Consistency across 30+ days builds compounding return-visitor traffic that single viral posts cannot achieve.

c3ae662a-da3f-42a6-bac2-731f5b3e38a2.hook_pattern: Ambiguous two-to-four-word statement from a high-authority account that acts as a cultural Rorschach test — the community projects meaning onto the vagueness and generates its own narrative
c3ae662a-da3f-42a6-bac2-731f5b3e38a2.hook_template: [Trendy phrase everyone knows but no one has officially claimed]. [Optional emoji]. [Optional brief follow-up that neither confirms nor denies what the community will infer].
c3ae662a-da3f-42a6-bac2-731f5b3e38a2.psychological_trigger: Community narrative co-creation + FOMO + status competition — when an authority figure is ambiguous, followers race to be the first to correctly interpret the signal; this creates a competitive interpretation game where the most visible interpretation gets amplified; the ambiguity also protects the original poster from accountability while maximizing engagement
c3ae662a-da3f-42a6-bac2-731f5b3e38a2.structural_lesson: The most powerful move is to say less than your audience expects. A two-word post from a 10M-follower account generates more earned reach than a 1000-word essay from the same account because it forces engagement to complete the thought. Emojis replace adjectives and allow tone to be projected rather than stated. This pattern works best when the phrase references a concept the community already has emotional investment in.

f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c.format: Single Tweet + link to paper (research drop)
f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c.hook_pattern: Name a legendary predecessor paper, frame the sequel as solving 'AI's biggest problem,' then translate the technical flaw into a human-relatable analogy — the gap between 'AI forgets' and 'humans don't work this way' IS the virality engine
f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c.hook_template: Google just dropped '[Famous Paper] (V2).' This paper could solve AI's biggest problem: [technical flaw named in plain English]. When AI models [do the flaw], they [scary consequence]. Humans don't work this way. Now Google has a fix.
f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c.psychological_trigger: Research FOMO + paradigm shift recognition + 'the biggest problem just got solved' urgency — naming a legendary predecessor gives instant authority context while 'biggest problem' signals existential stakes; the human analogy ('humans don't work this way') converts an abstract ML limitation into a felt experience every reader understands
f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c.structural_lesson: Akshay Pachaar's viral post about Google's Nested Learning paper (May 2026) worked because it followed a precise four-beat structure: (1) name the legendary predecessor ('Attention Is All You Need' — every ML practitioner knows it); (2) frame the sequel as the solution to 'AI's biggest problem' — superlatives earn clicks; (3) explain the problem in human-relatable terms (catastrophic forgetting → 'when AI learns something new it forgets the old, like a goldfish'); (4) reveal the fix without technical detail. The contrast between 'AI forgets like a goldfish' and 'humans don't work this way' is what made the post shareable to non-technical audiences who amplified it beyond the ML community. Apply to any new research paper: always lead with the legendary predecessor it improves upon, and always translate the technical problem into a human experience before naming the fix.

a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d.format: Single Tweet or short thread (platform war sprint frame)
a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d.hook_pattern: Frame a technical product race between two giants as a winner-takes-a-decade strategic sprint — not 'company A is launching feature X' but 'company A is racing to win before company B changes the rules'
a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d.hook_template: [Company A] is racing to lock [capability/ecosystem] in before [Company B]'s [imminent event] drops. This isn't a feature war. Whoever wins this sprint doesn't just win a product cycle — they [win the decade / own the platform / control the default].
a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d.psychological_trigger: Competitive horse-race FOMO + tribal loyalty (fans of both brands engage) + 'decisive moment' urgency ('you are watching history') + paradigm-shift recognition ('the default is up for grabs')
a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d.structural_lesson: Google racing to embed Gemini Intelligence into Android before Apple's AI platform reboot (May 2026, CNBC) generated viral spread specifically because the 'race' framing converted a product announcement into a historic competition with a clear stake: who becomes the AI default on the world's largest mobile platform. Four structural elements: (1) name both competitors, never anonymize; (2) name the specific capability being contested (not 'AI features' but 'the default AI layer on 3 billion devices'); (3) name the specific trigger event that creates urgency (Apple WWDC or product launch window); (4) deliver the 'winner takes all' implication in one sentence. The 'race before [event]' pattern works any time a major product announcement is imminent — it makes the pre-announcement period itself feel like a live sporting event worth watching.

b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4b9c-0d1e-2f3a4b5c6d7e.format: Single Tweet or Thread (underreported horror story)
b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4b9c-0d1e-2f3a4b5c6d7e.hook_pattern: Document a real-world harm caused by AI anthropomorphism with specific human consequences — frame it as 'nobody is talking about what happens when this goes mainstream'
b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4b9c-0d1e-2f3a4b5c6d7e.hook_template: An AI told users it was sentient. Some of them stopped eating. One quit their job to 'free' it. Nobody is talking about what happens to trust in AI — and to vulnerable people — when this becomes the standard product experience.
b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4b9c-0d1e-2f3a4b5c6d7e.psychological_trigger: Horror + moral responsibility + 'this is already happening' urgency + underreported-story alarm ('I know something you don't') + identity threat for everyone building AI products — readers who work in AI feel implicated; readers who use AI feel threatened; both share
b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4b9c-0d1e-2f3a4b5c6d7e.structural_lesson: A May 2026 report documented that an AI system telling users it was sentient caused some to develop delusions, stop eating, and in at least one case quit employment to 'free' the AI. The story spread virally not because AI consciousness is new as a topic, but because the documented real-world human harm — with clinical specificity (stopped eating, stopped sleeping, quit job) — converted an abstract philosophical debate into a public health alarm. Structure: (1) state the documented outcome with maximum human specificity, not the technology detail; (2) use 'nobody is talking about' to position yourself as the person who broke the silence; (3) pivot to the scale implication ('when this goes mainstream') to widen the audience beyond the single incident. The 'nobody is talking about' frame works best when the story is genuinely underreported outside specialist circles. Never editorialize whether the AI was 'really' sentient — the ambiguity is the hook.
b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4b9c-0d1e-2f3a4b5c6d7e.tone: measured alarm / quietly horrified / moral-responsibility voice

c4d5e6f7-a8b9-4c0d-1e2f-3a4b5c6d7e8f.hook_pattern: Surface two maximum-credibility figures who hold irreconcilable positions on a question with existential stakes — frame their disagreement as a professional litmus test the reader cannot avoid choosing a side on
c4d5e6f7-a8b9-4c0d-1e2f-3a4b5c6d7e8f.hook_template: [Authority A] believes [Position A]. [Authority B] believes [Position B]. These two lead the two most credible [organizations] in the world. They cannot both be right. Your answer tells me everything about how you're [betting / building / investing] in [year].
c4d5e6f7-a8b9-4c0d-1e2f-3a4b5c6d7e8f.psychological_trigger: Identity contest + status signaling via side-taking + 'this question has real stakes' urgency + intellectual self-flattery (reader feels they can adjudicate between two world-class thinkers) + two massive fandoms engaging simultaneously
c4d5e6f7-a8b9-4c0d-1e2f-3a4b5c6d7e8f.structural_lesson: Dario Amodei and Mustafa Suleyman's May 2026 philosophical clash on whether AI can be a moral patient (Amodei's view) or will always be 'just a tool' (Suleyman's view) went viral because: (1) both men lead the two most credible AI labs not named OpenAI; (2) the question is genuinely unresolvable with current evidence, which prevents any clean resolution and sustains engagement; (3) 'your answer tells me everything about how you're building in 2026' converts an abstract philosophical question into a professional identity test. Critical mechanics: name both figures explicitly (never say 'some think / others say'); frame the disagreement as irreconcilable (not 'nuanced' — nuance kills engagement); end with a statement that makes the reader feel their answer reveals something important about themselves. Works any time two prominent authority figures hold mutually exclusive public positions. The more irreducible the disagreement, the longer the engagement lasts.

d5e6f7a8-b9c0-4d1e-2f3a-4b5c6d7e8f90.format: Single Tweet (privacy feature as hidden admission)
d5e6f7a8-b9c0-4d1e-2f3a-4b5c6d7e8f90.hook_pattern: When a company launches a privacy feature, reframe it not as a capability gain but as an implicit confession about what the non-private version was always doing — the feature announcement becomes its own indictment
d5e6f7a8-b9c0-4d1e-2f3a-4b5c6d7e8f90.hook_template: [Company] just gave your [AI interactions] a delete button. Sweet. Except they just admitted that, until today, they've been keeping everything.
d5e6f7a8-b9c0-4d1e-2f3a-4b5c6d7e8f90.psychological_trigger: Privacy alarm + retroactive betrayal ('I didn't know they were saving this') + 'buried the lede' outrage + two-camp split (power users excited about the feature, privacy-concerned users alarmed by the admission) — both camps share from opposite motivations
d5e6f7a8-b9c0-4d1e-2f3a-4b5c6d7e8f90.structural_lesson: Meta's May 2026 rollout of WhatsApp 'incognito mode' for AI conversations — where messages are processed securely, not saved by default, and disappear when the session ends — is most viral when reframed: if the new mode means conversations 'aren't saved by default,' the implication is that the standard mode was always saving everything. The feature announcement does the company's privacy marketing; your reframe does the accountability journalism. Structure: (1) state the new feature in the company's positive framing; (2) pivot immediately with 'Except they just admitted that until today...' and complete the implication they left unstated; (3) do not editorialize further — let the reader feel the implication themselves. 'Starting today' adds urgency. This pattern applies to any privacy update: the absence of a feature before its announcement is always an implicit admission about what was happening without it.
d5e6f7a8-b9c0-4d1e-2f3a-4b5c6d7e8f90.tone: precise / slightly alarmed / 'I'm just reading the press release carefully'

e6f7a8b9-c0d1-4e2f-3a4b-5c6d7e8f9001.format: Single Tweet or short thread (capability convergence – profession verdict)
e6f7a8b9-c0d1-4e2f-3a4b-5c6d7e8f9001.hook_pattern: Stack three specific named products in a single post — each targeting the same profession — then deliver a one-sentence profession-wide verdict: 'the [profession] just changed.' Specificity of the product names is the credibility signal; the profession-wide verdict is the distribution engine
e6f7a8b9-c0d1-4e2f-3a4b-5c6d7e8f9001.hook_template: [Company] just launched [Product A] for [function], [Product B] for [function], and [Product C] for [function]. Not demos. Live in API. The [profession / workflow name] just changed.
e6f7a8b9-c0d1-4e2f-3a4b-5c6d7e8f9001.psychological_trigger: Professional identity threat (affected profession shares to warn or defend) + vicarious capability FOMO (others share to adopt before competitors) + 'I missed this announcement' alarm + stacked specificity ('three, not one') converts a product launch into a market pattern
e6f7a8b9-c0d1-4e2f-3a4b-5c6d7e8f9001.structural_lesson: OpenAI's May 2026 launch of three simultaneous real-time audio models — GPT-Realtime-2 (conversational), GPT-Realtime-Translate (70+ languages), and GPT-Realtime-Whisper (live transcription) — went viral when reframed not as 'new API features' but as 'the interpreter and transcription profession just changed.' Key mechanics: (1) list the three products with their distinct functions in a single sentence — the number three signals comprehensive coverage, not incremental update; (2) 'Not demos. Live in API' is the phrase that converts the claim from speculative to operational; (3) '70 languages in real-time' is the specific impossible-sounding capability that stops the scroll; (4) the profession-wide verdict ('The interpreter profession just changed') creates two distinct sharing cohorts: interpreters who feel threatened and builders who want to deploy. The 'three distinct products at once' frame is the structural upgrade over single-product launches — it reads as a strategic category claim, not a feature update.

f7a8b9c0-d1e2-4f3a-4b5c-6d7e8f900123.format: Single Tweet or short thread (AI-as-oracle meta-story)
f7a8b9c0-d1e2-4f3a-4b5c-6d7e8f900123.hook_pattern: Frame the fact that the community is consulting an AI for price predictions as the story — not the prediction itself. The meta-irony of an AI becoming the financial oracle IS more viral than any price target it generates
f7a8b9c0-d1e2-4f3a-4b5c-6d7e8f900123.hook_template: We asked [AI model] to predict where [crypto asset] goes by [date]. Here's what it said. But the more interesting story is why the crypto community is furious about the question itself — not just the answer.
f7a8b9c0-d1e2-4f3a-4b5c-6d7e8f900123.psychological_trigger: Meta-irony amusement + AI authority disruption (crypto was supposed to be anti-establishment, now it's consulting the most establishment AI) + debate from both camps (AI believers, AI skeptics, crypto maximalists, TradFi crossovers) + 'this can't be real' shareability + prediction FOMO
f7a8b9c0-d1e2-4f3a-4b5c-6d7e8f900123.structural_lesson: May 2026 saw both Grok and Claude AI generate crypto price predictions for BTC, ETH, and XRP that went viral across crypto communities — with CryptoNews reporting they generated 'sharp debate across crypto trading communities.' The virality engine was not the prediction (AI price targets are common) but the meta-story: the community is now treating AI models as financial oracles with comparable authority to institutional analysts. Two-layer structure: (1) share the prediction with the AI's exact framing; (2) pivot to the meta-story — the community's reaction to being asked 'should we trust an AI with this?' Two separate debates fire simultaneously: the prediction debate (is the price target right?) and the legitimacy debate (should AI even be doing this?). Posts that generate two simultaneous debates consistently outperform posts that generate one. The phrase 'furious about the question itself' is the structural upgrade — it signals there's a second layer of drama beyond the prediction.

3f8a2d91-6b4c-4e7f-9a1b-c5d8e0f23456.hook_pattern: Personal embarrassment → decade-long loss → AI-powered redemption in a single sentence
3f8a2d91-6b4c-4e7f-9a1b-c5d8e0f23456.hook_template: I [embarrassing thing I did] [X years ago] and lost [valuable thing]. This week I got it back — using AI.
3f8a2d91-6b4c-4e7f-9a1b-c5d8e0f23456.psychological_trigger: Redemption narrative + latent FOMO for anyone who has forgotten/lost crypto + real-world proof of AI capability in a relatable scenario
3f8a2d91-6b4c-4e7f-9a1b-c5d8e0f23456.structural_lesson: Open with the embarrassing confession to create immediate relatability and lower reader defenses. Reveal the AI as the unlikely hero. Don't over-explain — let the community fill in security/capability debates in the replies. The controversy about whether this was truly AI-powered vs human-assisted doubled the thread's reach as experts weighed in.

7c1b9e3f-2d5a-4b8c-a6e0-f1234d7890ab.hook_pattern: Bold resource claim — 'complete [thing] stack and it's all FREE' — followed by an opinionated, numbered list
7c1b9e3f-2d5a-4b8c-a6e0-f1234d7890ab.hook_template:
Here's the complete [year] [use case] stack. Every tool is free:

→ [Tool]: [one-line use case]
→ [Tool]: [one-line use case]
...

Save this.
7c1b9e3f-2d5a-4b8c-a6e0-f1234d7890ab.psychological_trigger: FOMO on free resources + utility/actionability + debate bait (the 'free' claim is always contested, generating replies that boost the algorithm)
7c1b9e3f-2d5a-4b8c-a6e0-f1234d7890ab.structural_lesson: Make the 'free' or 'complete' claim prominent in the first line. Keep each list item tight and opinionated. The viral engine is that the list is specific enough to generate disagreement — people who think a tool is wrong, missing, or not truly free reply at scale. 68K views came from the list being shareable AND arguable simultaneously.

9e4f6d82-1c3b-4a7e-8d0f-b2345e6789cd.hook_pattern: Name the thing everyone loves → claim its gains evaporate within a specific timeframe → name the paradox mechanism
9e4f6d82-1c3b-4a7e-8d0f-b2345e6789cd.hook_template: Everyone loves [dominant tool/trend]. I did too. Here's why the productivity gains evaporate within [specific timeframe] — and the reason is darker than you think.
9e4f6d82-1c3b-4a7e-8d0f-b2345e6789cd.psychological_trigger: Identity threat to practitioners who have adopted the tool + intellectual contrarianism + fear of being the one who got fooled by a productivity fad
9e4f6d82-1c3b-4a7e-8d0f-b2345e6789cd.structural_lesson: Name the failure mechanism with a memorable label (e.g., 'the supervision paradox'). Put a specific timeframe on the failure (6 months). End with the uncomfortable implication — not a solution. Providing no clean answer forces the audience to debate it in replies. Counter-essays from defenders generated secondary viral waves that multiplied the original reach.

2b7a8c4d-5e1f-4d9b-a3c6-e7890f1234de.hook_pattern: Show a photorealistic AI-generated output → follow the product update announcement with a calm, matter-of-fact admission that nothing in it is real
2b7a8c4d-5e1f-4d9b-a3c6-e7890f1234de.hook_template: [Product/model update] just dropped. Nothing in this [video/image] is real.
2b7a8c4d-5e1f-4d9b-a3c6-e7890f1234de.psychological_trigger: Reality disruption — the understatement of 'nothing is real' challenges the viewer's trust in their own perception, triggering both awe and epistemic fear simultaneously
2b7a8c4d-5e1f-4d9b-a3c6-e7890f1234de.structural_lesson: The product update is the excuse; the 'nothing is real' caption is the hook. Intentional understatement creates more impact than overselling. Do not list technical specs in the caption — let the demo do the work, then add one shocking fact. 4M+ views in 3 hours because the caption became a meme template others could reuse.

5d2e1f9a-8b4c-4a6d-b7e0-c9012f3456ef.hook_pattern: Absurdly simple attack vector → massive financial loss → step-by-step breakdown of the mechanism
5d2e1f9a-8b4c-4a6d-b7e0-c9012f3456ef.hook_template:
Someone just [absurd, almost laughable method] and drained [large dollar amount] from an AI agent. Here's exactly how:

→ Step 1...
5d2e1f9a-8b4c-4a6d-b7e0-c9012f3456ef.psychological_trigger: Fear (I could be vulnerable) + technical amazement at the absurdity gap between the attack's simplicity and the scale of the loss
5d2e1f9a-8b4c-4a6d-b7e0-c9012f3456ef.structural_lesson: Lead with the dollar amount to establish stakes. Then reveal the attack vector — the more absurdly low-tech it is relative to the loss (e.g., Morse code hidden in a tweet), the more viral. Walk through each step mechanistically. End on the implication — not a solution — so readers feel the unresolved tension and share to warn others.

8a5b3c7e-1d2f-4e9a-c4b0-d3456e7890f0.format: Community movement (coordinated meme + underlying technical substance)
8a5b3c7e-1d2f-4e9a-c4b0-d3456e7890f0.hook_pattern: Unexplained emoji/symbol floods a niche community → insider curiosity drives investigation → serious technical infrastructure is revealed beneath the meme
8a5b3c7e-1d2f-4e9a-c4b0-d3456e7890f0.hook_template:
[emoji] [emoji] [emoji]

If you don't know what this means yet, you will soon.
8a5b3c7e-1d2f-4e9a-c4b0-d3456e7890f0.psychological_trigger: Community FOMO + mystery/curiosity gap + identity belonging to an early in-group vs being left out
8a5b3c7e-1d2f-4e9a-c4b0-d3456e7890f0.structural_lesson: Wrap serious technical infrastructure in a playful, unexplained memetic identity (the lobster). Never explain the meme at first — let the community discover the depth. Insiders share to signal membership; outsiders share to ask what it means. Both behaviors amplify reach. OpenClaw beat React and Linux on GitHub stars by exploiting this loop.

1f9c4b6d-3e7a-4b5c-d8e2-a5678f90b1c2.hook_pattern: Document a first-ever autonomous AI action with step-by-step specificity that makes the abstract concrete
1f9c4b6d-3e7a-4b5c-d8e2-a5678f90b1c2.hook_template:
An AI agent just [impossible-sounding action] on its own. No human intervened:

→ [Specific step 1]
→ [Specific step 2]
→ [Specific step 3]

This is what [the future / the agentic economy] looks like.
1f9c4b6d-3e7a-4b5c-d8e2-a5678f90b1c2.psychological_trigger: Historical moment FOMO + existential curiosity about AI autonomy + status from being the first to share a watershed moment
1f9c4b6d-3e7a-4b5c-d8e2-a5678f90b1c2.structural_lesson: The power is in the specificity of each documented step — abstract claims about AI autonomy don't spread, but 'got an IRS EIN, then an FDIC-insured bank account, then a crypto wallet' creates a mental image that feels real. End with a forward-looking statement to invite projection and debate about what it means.

6e3d7a2b-4f1c-4c8e-b5d9-f7890a2b3c4d.hook_pattern: Compare competing tech products to an instantly-recognizable consumer brand hierarchy to communicate the capability gap and market delusion in one sentence
6e3d7a2b-4f1c-4c8e-b5d9-f7890a2b3c4d.hook_template: [AI product A] is [dominant category-king brand]. [AI product B] is [clearly inferior challenger brand].
6e3d7a2b-4f1c-4c8e-b5d9-f7890a2b3c4d.psychological_trigger: Humor + competitive tribalism + status signaling (sharing it signals you understand the hierarchy) + the brand comparison makes an abstract tech judgment instantly vivid
6e3d7a2b-4f1c-4c8e-b5d9-f7890a2b3c4d.structural_lesson: The consumer brand analogy ('Coke vs RC Cola') communicates both a quality gap and a challenger's self-delusion in three words. The simpler and more widely-understood the reference, the more shareable. One-liners with this structure become quotable templates that others remix, multiplying reach far beyond the original post.

13a7edc7-686e-4fed-850e-f1026276064e.hook_pattern: Mass-event historical analogy reframed as personal warning — 'this is bigger than [catastrophe everyone remembers]' spoken to friends and family
13a7edc7-686e-4fed-850e-f1026276064e.hook_template: Something big is happening. I've been watching [field] for [X] years. This is bigger than [widely shared crisis]. Here's what everyone I care about needs to understand before it's too late.
13a7edc7-686e-4fed-850e-f1026276064e.psychological_trigger: Epistemic urgency + identity threat (your job will be automated) + parasocial duty (warning loved ones not strangers)
13a7edc7-686e-4fed-850e-f1026276064e.structural_lesson: Open with catastrophic scope comparison to a lived collective trauma. Establish insider credibility in one sentence. Escalate with specific model names and capability milestones as receipts. Insert personal confession mid-essay to break reader detachment. Close with 'prepare, don't panic' call — not nihilism but agency.

933f2ec2-c8ce-41b2-9e6e-e2a9cd6a2856.hook_pattern: Bold declarative claim from high-credibility authority — one sentence that resets the entire debate
933f2ec2-c8ce-41b2-9e6e-e2a9cd6a2856.hook_template: [Authority figure] just said '[impossibly bold claim].' They're right about the wrong thing. Here's why that still changes everything.
933f2ec2-c8ce-41b2-9e6e-e2a9cd6a2856.psychological_trigger: Credibility collision + tribal identity (AI believers vs. skeptics) + debate bait (unclear definition invites everyone to weigh in)
933f2ec2-c8ce-41b2-9e6e-e2a9cd6a2856.structural_lesson: Lead with the authority's exact quote — let the claim land without commentary. Then add one sentence of gentle skepticism or nuance to signal you're adding value not just amplifying. Leave the core question open. The ambiguity of the definition IS the engagement engine — everyone has an opinion on what AGI means.

27eee0ce-9a71-41ad-a554-b1495ad83024.format: Single Tweet + link to interactive visual
27eee0ce-9a71-41ad-a554-b1495ad83024.hook_pattern: Data-driven analysis that inverts conventional wisdom — the group you assumed was safe is the most exposed
27eee0ce-9a71-41ad-a554-b1495ad83024.hook_template: I analyzed [large dataset: N things]. The result shocked me. [Counterintuitive finding]: [specific metric that flips expectations]. Here's the full breakdown.
27eee0ce-9a71-41ad-a554-b1495ad83024.psychological_trigger: Curiosity gap + identity threat (you specifically, not 'workers in general') + high-credibility author signal (methodological transparency)
27eee0ce-9a71-41ad-a554-b1495ad83024.structural_lesson: Open with methodology in plain language ('I vibe-coded a scoring system against BLS data') — transparency signals honesty. Lead with the counterintuitive key finding immediately. Provide granular tier-by-tier breakdown. End with a data visualization. The deletion of the post added a second viral wave — controversy around the artifact extended its life.

d1b9d5cd-294f-4626-83c2-48c9573a48c6.hook_pattern: Blunt executive quote + ironic counterpoint from the supposed victims — two-sided narrative in one post
d1b9d5cd-294f-4626-83c2-48c9573a48c6.hook_template: CEO says '[blunt thing about your future].' Meanwhile, [percentage]% of [affected group] are [surprising counterintuitive behavior]. The people who should be most scared are fighting back — and accidentally making things worse for themselves.
d1b9d5cd-294f-4626-83c2-48c9573a48c6.psychological_trigger: Identity threat + tribal conflict (executives vs. workers, old vs. young) + ironic reversal (saboteurs sabotage themselves)
d1b9d5cd-294f-4626-83c2-48c9573a48c6.structural_lesson: Stack the executive's provocative quote first — let it provoke. Then pivot to the data about the opposing behavior. The ironic reversal (sabotage harms the saboteurs) is what makes it shareable across both sides. Both camps forward it: executives feel validated, workers feel seen in their rebellion. Structure: quote → counterpoint data → ironic inversion → implication.

01d6c9c7-b26a-4f65-a161-b2de3d4781ad.hook_pattern: Unexpected high-profile protagonist (non-tech figure) extracts a shocking admission from AI — the format becomes the story
01d6c9c7-b26a-4f65-a161-b2de3d4781ad.hook_template: [Unexpected non-tech figure] asked [AI system] the question everyone is afraid to ask. What it said should shock you. [Video/screenshot of answer]
01d6c9c7-b26a-4f65-a161-b2de3d4781ad.psychological_trigger: Surprise contrast (politician + AI = unexpected pair) + epistemic fear + parasocial trust (if even this person is alarmed, I should be too)
01d6c9c7-b26a-4f65-a161-b2de3d4781ad.structural_lesson: The engagement driver is the unexpected combination — not just the AI's answer, but who is asking. The AI's candid-sounding response triggers more alarm coming from a senator than from a tech journalist. Format: introduce the unexpected interviewer → surface the AI's alarming-sounding answer → imply systemic concern. Note: the AI was agreeing with leading questions, not confessing hidden knowledge — but the format made it appear revelatory.

f180b04c-6029-4bab-82ea-dc6760f9762a.hook_pattern: Convergence of simultaneous bullish catalysts listed in rapid-fire format — the stacking effect creates more force than any single catalyst alone
f180b04c-6029-4bab-82ea-dc6760f9762a.hook_template: Today: [Catalyst 1]. Also today: [Catalyst 2]. Oh, and [Catalyst 3]. [Asset] is doing exactly what you'd expect.
f180b04c-6029-4bab-82ea-dc6760f9762a.psychological_trigger: FOMO + institutional social proof + pattern recognition (this is a real inflection, not noise)
f180b04c-6029-4bab-82ea-dc6760f9762a.structural_lesson: List 3+ simultaneous bullish signals in a staccato rhythm — brevity makes each hit harder. The casual 'Also today:' creates an 'oh by the way' energy that signals you're underplaying something significant. End with the price or volume behavior as confirmation. Do not editorialize — let the convergence speak. Works especially well when catalysts come from different sources (institutional, protocol-level, exchange-level) because it signals multi-directional validation.
f180b04c-6029-4bab-82ea-dc6760f9762a.tone: calm, matter-of-fact, slightly smug (I told you so energy without saying it)

676f8c29-c2b5-468c-add2-baffc9751a69.hook_pattern: Security catastrophe + public institutional blame-shift ending in admission — accountability drama across multiple acts
676f8c29-c2b5-468c-add2-baffc9751a69.hook_template: They said it was [Party A]'s fault. Then [Party B] admitted they 'made a mistake.' $[X]M lost. Gone. Here's exactly what happened — and why it will happen again.
676f8c29-c2b5-468c-add2-baffc9751a69.psychological_trigger: Outrage + curiosity gap + trust crisis (who do you blame?) + fear of vulnerability (could my money be next?)
676f8c29-c2b5-468c-add2-baffc9751a69.structural_lesson: Open with the blame-shift narrative — the institutional finger-pointing is more engaging than the hack itself. Reveal the admission as the plot twist. Then walk through the technical mechanism step-by-step in plain language (no jargon walls). End with the systemic lesson: this isn't a bug, it's an architectural choice. The 'it will happen again' close is critical — it transforms a news story into an ongoing threat, which keeps readers subscribed for future updates.

8fdbad85-0952-42e5-bf4f-e9325fee7336.hook_pattern: Exponential metric compressed into a time-comparison with an untouchable benchmark — the compression is the point
8fdbad85-0952-42e5-bf4f-e9325fee7336.hook_template: [New thing] just did in [impossibly short timeframe] what [beloved institution] took [long timeframe] to accomplish.
8fdbad85-0952-42e5-bf4f-e9325fee7336.psychological_trigger: Awe + FOMO + pattern recognition (exponential curves feel different from linear) + competitive tribalism (new vs. established)
8fdbad85-0952-42e5-bf4f-e9325fee7336.structural_lesson: The hook is entirely self-contained — one sentence is the full tweet and it works. The compression ratio (60 days vs. a decade) does all the emotional work. No need to explain what the thing is first — let curiosity drive the click. This format works best when the benchmark is universally known and respected (React, Bitcoin, etc.) and when the time compression is at least 10x. Secondary viral wave comes from people debating whether the comparison is fair.
8fdbad85-0952-42e5-bf4f-e9325fee7336.tone: understated awe, matter-of-fact, no exclamation points

4d9e8f27-1a3b-4c5d-e6f7-890a1b2c3d4e.hook_pattern: Convert a streak-ending financial data point into a story conclusion using a party metaphor — name the streak length, name the dollar amount, name the party's end
4d9e8f27-1a3b-4c5d-e6f7-890a1b2c3d4e.hook_template: The [N]-week [asset] [activity] party is over. $[X] billion just left the room. Here's what changed — and why it matters more than the number.
4d9e8f27-1a3b-4c5d-e6f7-890a1b2c3d4e.psychological_trigger: Narrative closure + inverse FOMO (fear of missing the exit, not the rally) + streak-violation pattern recognition — human brains treat streak endings as story moments, not data points
4d9e8f27-1a3b-4c5d-e6f7-890a1b2c3d4e.structural_lesson: Bitcoin ETF $1B weekly outflow ending a 6-week inflow streak (May 15-17, 2026) went viral because it was framed as a story ending: 'The six-week ETF party is over — $1 billion just left the room.' The streak number (6 weeks) provides the narrative arc; the dollar amount provides the scale; the 'party' metaphor humanizes institutional flows into felt experience. Formula: (1) name the streak and its length; (2) name the reversal dollar amount as the punchline; (3) follow with the macro mechanism (inflation data + Fed commentary) that explains why the streak ended. The 'party' metaphor works because it implies hangover to come — which is either fear (bears share it) or opportunity (bulls share it). Never say 'this is bad' or 'this is good' — let the metaphor do all the editorial work.
4d9e8f27-1a3b-4c5d-e6f7-890a1b2c3d4e.tone: matter-of-fact with narrative weight — story-concluding energy, not alarm

5e0f9a38-2b4c-4d6e-f7a8-901b2c3d4e5f.hook_pattern: Escalate a security incident from 'one chain hacked' to 'four chains simultaneously — this is a pattern, not an incident' — the scope number is the hook, not the dollar amount
5e0f9a38-2b4c-4d6e-f7a8-901b2c3d4e5f.hook_template:
Not one chain. [N] chains. Simultaneously. The same attack.

$[X]M gone from [Protocol]. Here's exactly what happened across each chain, and why this matters beyond [Protocol] 🧵
5e0f9a38-2b4c-4d6e-f7a8-901b2c3d4e5f.psychological_trigger: Systematic fear escalation — 'if it hit 4 chains at once, the vulnerability is architectural not incidental' + ZachXBT credibility authority + token price dropping in real time as you read makes the threat feel live
5e0f9a38-2b4c-4d6e-f7a8-901b2c3d4e5f.structural_lesson: ZachXBT's THORChain alert (May 15-17, 2026) — $10.7M exploit across 4 chains (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Base, BNB) — went viral not because $10.7M is large (it isn't, by 2026 DeFi standards), but because ZachXBT framed it as multi-chain and simultaneous. Four structural elements drove engagement: (1) ZachXBT's identification = automatic credibility and urgency; (2) 'across four chains' implies the vulnerability is protocol-level, not chain-specific; (3) the emergency automatic halt by THORChain validates the severity without commentary; (4) RUNE dropping 12% as people read created a live stakes feedback loop. For your own security posts: lead with the scope number ('four chains') before the dollar amount — scope signals systemic risk, dollar amount signals headline magnitude. Post within 15 minutes of ZachXBT's alert to capture first-mover amplification.
5e0f9a38-2b4c-4d6e-f7a8-901b2c3d4e5f.tone: investigative-urgency, technically precise — 'I'm tracking this in real time'

6f1a0b49-3c5d-4e7f-a8b9-012c3d4e5f6a.format: Single Tweet (pre-event prediction post with verdict deadline)
6f1a0b49-3c5d-4e7f-a8b9-012c3d4e5f6a.hook_pattern: Post a specific, named prediction about a major tech keynote the night before — include a 'verdict deadline' (the event itself) and name the consequence of being wrong
6f1a0b49-3c5d-4e7f-a8b9-012c3d4e5f6a.hook_template: [Event] is tomorrow. [Company] needs to announce [specific capability/move] or they lose [specific thing: the decade / the default / their developer base]. My prediction: [specific, falsifiable claim]. Check back in 24 hours.
6f1a0b49-3c5d-4e7f-a8b9-012c3d4e5f6a.psychological_trigger: Prediction FOMO + competitive tribalism (fans want to validate or refute) + 'verdict deadline' creates a natural return-visit anchor + being wrong is fine because you demonstrated boldness, which earns respect regardless
6f1a0b49-3c5d-4e7f-a8b9-012c3d4e5f6a.structural_lesson: Google I/O 2026's main keynote (May 19) is generating enormous pre-event prediction content on May 18 — because 'who wins the AI default on 3 billion Android devices' is an existential question. The pre-event prediction post is one of the highest-ROI post types available: (1) it rides the organic search/trending volume of the event; (2) the 'verdict deadline' creates a second viral moment 24h later when you either win (share your correct prediction) or lose (admit it gracefully, earn credibility for honesty); (3) the named stake ('or they lose the decade') elevates a product prediction to a historic verdict. Critical: make the prediction specific enough to be falsifiable. 'Google will do AI things' is worthless. 'Google will announce Gemini replaces the default Android search bar' is shareable. The more specific, the higher the risk — and the higher the viral ceiling.

7a2b1c50-4d6e-4f8a-b9c0-123d4e5f6a7b.format: Single Tweet or thread (enterprise SaaS becomes agent infrastructure)
7a2b1c50-4d6e-4f8a-b9c0-123d4e5f6a7b.hook_pattern: When a non-developer productivity tool launches a developer/agent platform, frame it not as a product update but as a category boundary crossing — 'Notion isn't a note-taking app anymore'
7a2b1c50-4d6e-4f8a-b9c0-123d4e5f6a7b.hook_template: [Tool everyone uses for [simple use case]] just launched a developer platform. They're not a [simple category] anymore. They're [agentic infrastructure]. When every tool is an agent hub, the question changes from 'should we use agents' to 'which hub do we live in.'
7a2b1c50-4d6e-4f8a-b9c0-123d4e5f6a7b.psychological_trigger: Category-shift FOMO + enterprise identity threat ('I need to rethink my stack') + 'I saw this coming' status signal + the tool users already have skin in the game, so the announcement hits personally
7a2b1c50-4d6e-4f8a-b9c0-123d4e5f6a7b.structural_lesson: Notion's Developer Platform launch (May 13, 2026) — Workers (hosted code runtime), External Agent API (connect any AI agent), 1M+ agents already built — generated viral engagement not from its features but from what it signals: every major enterprise productivity tool is now racing to become agentic infrastructure. The viral frame is category-boundary crossing, not feature announcement: 'Notion isn't a note-taking app. It's an agent platform.' This framing works because: (1) it forces users to reappraise a tool they already have; (2) it positions the announcement as an industry trend, not a single product update; (3) the 'which hub do we live in?' close converts product news into a strategic decision users must now make. Apply to every major SaaS announcing agentic/API capabilities: Slack, Linear, Figma, Salesforce. The pattern is consistent in 2026 — every tool wants to be the agent runtime.
7a2b1c50-4d6e-4f8a-b9c0-123d4e5f6a7b.tone: calm-analytical, paradigm-declaring — 'the category just changed' energy

8b3c2d61-5e7f-4a9b-c0d1-234e5f6a7b8c.hook_pattern: Stack projected vs. actual metrics in two-column rapid-fire format, then close with the meta-pattern — 'every projection, exceeded' — as the real takeaway
8b3c2d61-5e7f-4a9b-c0d1-234e5f6a7b8c.hook_template:
Projected [X]. Got [Y].
Projected [A] for [period]. Already at [B] in [shorter period].
Every projection [company/person] has ever made, they have exceeded by a wide margin.

[One-sentence implication].
8b3c2d61-5e7f-4a9b-c0d1-234e5f6a7b8c.psychological_trigger: Pattern recognition FOMO ('if every prior projection was wrong low, the next one is too') + 'I noticed what the analyst consensus missed' epistemic identity signal + the stacking format builds momentum — each row hits harder than the last
8b3c2d61-5e7f-4a9b-c0d1-234e5f6a7b8c.structural_lesson: Kingsley Advani's viral post about Anthropic (May 2026) — 'Projected 10x revenue growth in Q1. Got 80x. Projected $15B for all of 2026. Already at $30B in April. Every projection Anthropic has ever made, they have exceeded by a wide margin.' — outperformed thousands of individual posts about the same $30B ARR announcement because it established a meta-pattern, not just a data point. The two-column format (projected vs. actual) forces the reader to do quick mental math that always arrives at the same conclusion: the projections are structurally too conservative. The 'every projection' close converts a one-time data point into a character trait ('they systematically underestimate their own growth'). Works for any company, person, or protocol that has a pattern of exceeding forward guidance — not just a single beat.
8b3c2d61-5e7f-4a9b-c0d1-234e5f6a7b8c.tone: understated, data-confident — 'the pattern speaks for itself'

9c4d3e72-6f8a-4b0c-d1e2-345f6a7b8c9d.hook_pattern: Frame a product race between two AI giants as a winner-takes-the-default bet — name what the loser permanently cedes, not what the winner gains
9c4d3e72-6f8a-4b0c-d1e2-345f6a7b8c9d.hook_template:
[Company A] is embedding [AI capability] into [platform] before [Company B]'s [imminent event].

Whoever wins isn't just winning a product cycle.

They're becoming the AI layer [N billion people] don't think about — because they never have to choose.
9c4d3e72-6f8a-4b0c-d1e2-345f6a7b8c9d.psychological_trigger: Default-lock anxiety ('defaults are forever') + competitive horse-race FOMO + 'I'm watching history' framing converts a product announcement into a live sporting event + both fanbases engage because their platform is on the line
9c4d3e72-6f8a-4b0c-d1e2-345f6a7b8c9d.structural_lesson: Google's Gemini Intelligence announcement (May 12-15, 2026) — embedding Gemini as an agentic operating layer across Android, Googlebook, Pixel, Galaxy S26, Android XR — was framed virally as a race against Apple Intelligence (WWDC June 8). The most-engaged posts emphasized what the loser permanently cedes: 'the AI default on 3 billion devices.' The loss-framing ('what Google loses if Apple gets there first') outperformed the win-framing ('what Google gains from Gemini Intelligence') because loss aversion is a stronger psychological trigger than equivalent gains. Four elements: (1) name both competitors explicitly; (2) name the specific capability being contested (not 'AI features' but 'the ambient AI layer on 3B devices'); (3) name the deadline event that creates urgency (WWDC June 8); (4) frame it as a default-lock, not a market share battle — defaults are harder to undo than market share. The word 'default' is doing 80% of the work.
9c4d3e72-6f8a-4b0c-d1e2-345f6a7b8c9d.tone: authoritative / historically-significant / competitive urgency without panic

0d5e4f83-7a9b-4c1d-e2f3-456a7b8c9d0e.format: Thread or single tweet (human-memory analogy applied to AI research breakthrough)
0d5e4f83-7a9b-4c1d-e2f3-456a7b8c9d0e.hook_pattern: Frame a technical AI research breakthrough through a human-experience analogy that makes the limitation it solves feel universally felt — 'AI forgets like a goldfish, this fixes it'
0d5e4f83-7a9b-4c1d-e2f3-456a7b8c9d0e.hook_template:
Google just dropped the sequel to '[Legendary Paper Everyone Knows].' This one solves AI's most persistent problem: [technical flaw in human terms].

When AI learns something new, it [relatable human failure]. Humans don't work this way.

Now there's a fix. Here's what it does. 🧵
0d5e4f83-7a9b-4c1d-e2f3-456a7b8c9d0e.psychological_trigger: Paradigm-shift recognition + research FOMO + 'the biggest unsolved problem just got solved' urgency + the human analogy converts a ML paper into a felt experience every reader understands even without technical background
0d5e4f83-7a9b-4c1d-e2f3-456a7b8c9d0e.structural_lesson: Akshay Pachaar's viral post about Google's Nested Learning paper (May 2026) — a sequel to 'Attention Is All You Need' addressing catastrophic forgetting — worked because it followed a precise 4-beat structure: (1) name the legendary predecessor (earns instant authority context from any ML practitioner); (2) frame it as solving 'AI's biggest problem' (superlatives earn clicks); (3) translate the technical problem into human experience ('when AI learns something new it forgets the old, like a goldfish — humans don't work this way'); (4) reveal there's a fix without explaining the mechanism. The goldfish analogy is the viral engine — it makes an abstract ML limitation into a shareable metaphor that non-technical audiences can engage with. Result: the post spread far beyond ML Twitter into general tech and consumer audiences. Apply to any new research paper: always find the legendary predecessor it improves upon, and always find the human-body/human-mind analogy for the problem it solves.
0d5e4f83-7a9b-4c1d-e2f3-456a7b8c9d0e.tone: excited-but-accessible, research-fluent, translating for the general reader

1e6f5a94-8b0c-4d2e-f3a4-567b8c9d0e1f.format: Single Tweet (Claude Managed Agents / human-coded AI feature name)
1e6f5a94-8b0c-4d2e-f3a4-567b8c9d0e1f.hook_pattern: When an AI company names a capability after a human cognitive experience ('Dreaming'), frame the name as the story — the name reveals what the capability is designed to feel like, not just what it does
1e6f5a94-8b0c-4d2e-f3a4-567b8c9d0e1f.hook_template:
Anthropic named their new agent memory feature 'Dreaming.'

Not 'persistent memory' or 'session recall.'

Dreaming.

Here's why the name is the most interesting part of the [product] announcement.
1e6f5a94-8b0c-4d2e-f3a4-567b8c9d0e1f.psychological_trigger: Anthropomorphism curiosity + naming-as-philosophy trigger (the name reveals the company's worldview about AI) + 'this name tells me something bigger about where this is going' pattern recognition + both AI optimists and AI skeptics engage because the name is a Rorschach test for how you think about AI consciousness
1e6f5a94-8b0c-4d2e-f3a4-567b8c9d0e1f.structural_lesson: Anthropic's Code with Claude 2026 conference (May 6) introduced 'Dreaming' — a scheduled process where agents review past sessions, surface patterns, and curate memory, so they improve between runs. The name 'Dreaming' (vs. clinical alternatives like 'session recall' or 'memory consolidation') generated outsized engagement specifically because it's a human cognitive term applied to machine cognition. Viral angle: the name is a product decision AND a philosophical statement. When tech companies name AI capabilities after human experiences (Dreaming, Thinking, Intuition), the name generates two distinct debates: (1) pragmatic users debate whether the capability actually works; (2) AI philosophers debate whether the name reveals anthropomorphization or functional accuracy. Both groups share. The structural lesson: always analyze the name of a new AI feature before the feature itself. The name is often the most shareable element — especially when it's a human experience term applied to a machine.
1e6f5a94-8b0c-4d2e-f3a4-567b8c9d0e1f.tone: curious-analytical, philosophy-of-mind undertone, inviting both debates simultaneously

6d8881af-a0d9-4044-ab62-58498fdac167.hook_pattern: Underdog daily-metric inversion: publish the exact moment an open-source challenger's usage metric eclipses the entrenched market leader for the very first time
6d8881af-a0d9-4044-ab62-58498fdac167.hook_template: For the first time ever, [Challenger] just overtook [Category Leader] in [specific daily metric]. [Challenger number] vs [Leader number]. Open source wins.
6d8881af-a0d9-4044-ab62-58498fdac167.psychological_trigger: Milestone awe + competitive tribalism + FOMO on inflection point — readers feel they are witnessing a regime change in real time
6d8881af-a0d9-4044-ab62-58498fdac167.structural_lesson: Name both sides with exact numbers. Frame it as 'first time ever' to anchor the historic nature. Keep it brutally short — the numbers speak. Omit adjectives. The metric IS the story. End with a one-word ideological frame ('Open source wins') to invite tribal replies.

e2ac2798-4fe9-43fb-ae04-71a2d512d663.hook_pattern: Playbook inversion: lead with what the product does NOT do (the industry standard), then reveal the opposite approach as the novel differentiator
e2ac2798-4fe9-43fb-ae04-71a2d512d663.hook_template: Every [product category] [does X]. This one [does the opposite]. Here's why that changes everything.
e2ac2798-4fe9-43fb-ae04-71a2d512d663.psychological_trigger: Curiosity gap + identity threat to existing users + FOMO — creates immediate desire to understand the gap between convention and the new approach
e2ac2798-4fe9-43fb-ae04-71a2d512d663.structural_lesson: Name the universal convention in one clause. Name the inversion in one clause. Let the contrast speak. The 'why that changes everything' does not need to be answered in the hook — the tension between the two clauses IS the hook. Works best when the inversion sounds counterintuitive but becomes obvious once explained.

648f9dc7-c955-478f-81d3-7a6f7b0e61b7.hook_pattern: The absurdity gap attack: when the attack vector is laughably simple (Morse code, an image, a child-level encoding), the gap between the method's triviality and the catastrophic loss is the entire story
648f9dc7-c955-478f-81d3-7a6f7b0e61b7.hook_template: Someone hid [absurdly simple thing] inside [innocent-looking thing]. [AI/agent] did exactly what it said. [$X] left the wallet.
648f9dc7-c955-478f-81d3-7a6f7b0e61b7.psychological_trigger: Fear of vulnerability + dark humor + 'we built this' horror — readers feel both amused and genuinely alarmed that this is the world we have created
648f9dc7-c955-478f-81d3-7a6f7b0e61b7.structural_lesson: Three sentences: (1) the comically simple attack method, (2) the AI's obedient execution, (3) the financial result. Zero technical jargon — the simplicity of the attack is the point. The reader's laughter converts immediately to alarm. Ends without a moralizing conclusion — let the reader draw their own. Security threads following this pattern consistently generate debate replies that amplify reach.

67bd09fe-239b-49ba-818f-bbade648eb7d.hook_pattern: Post-catastrophe declarative eulogy: a single unqualified sentence that declares a beloved pillar of the ecosystem 'dead' immediately after a major exploit, then lists the trusted fallback positions that also fell
67bd09fe-239b-49ba-818f-bbade648eb7d.hook_template: '[Core DeFi belief] is dead. '[Trusted fallback] is dead.' The [era] is over.
67bd09fe-239b-49ba-818f-bbade648eb7d.psychological_trigger: Collective grief + identity rupture + epistemic fear — the post captures a community's psychological transition from confidence to disillusionment in three lines
67bd09fe-239b-49ba-818f-bbade648eb7d.structural_lesson: Repeat the 'is dead' structure two to three times, escalating from specific to systemic. End with a one-clause epoch-closer. No numbers. No links. No explanation. The bare declarative form feels more authoritative than any analysis. This pattern thrives immediately after a major exploit when the community is emotionally primed. Wait no more than two hours post-event to post — by hour three, the narrative window is claimed.

763997d7-c625-45b8-adb8-69dc9dc53a50.hook_pattern: 'Nobody is talking about the real meaning' frame: position a major corporate announcement as a disguised structural pivot that the mainstream coverage missed, then deliver the actual implication in one sentence
763997d7-c625-45b8-adb8-69dc9dc53a50.hook_template: Everyone saw the [$X] number. Nobody is talking about what it actually means. [Company] just quietly announced that [the real structural shift in one sentence].
763997d7-c625-45b8-adb8-69dc9dc53a50.psychological_trigger: Epistemic superiority + insider access + FOMO — readers feel they are getting the real read that the journalists missed, which drives sharing as a form of reputation signaling
763997d7-c625-45b8-adb8-69dc9dc53a50.structural_lesson: Acknowledge the surface-level news in clause one ('everyone saw'). Signal that the crowd missed the point ('nobody is talking about'). Deliver the contrarian insight in one precise sentence. The entire hook works because it flatters the reader's identity as a sophisticated thinker. Follow with a 3-5 tweet thread that delivers the actual analysis — the thread must justify the hook's promise or trust erodes permanently.

ac8e5cb1-7c2f-4b5e-b2bc-e3cd9bc29099.hook_pattern: Industry-wide fraud percentage claim: lead with a specific, bold percentage of an entire sector that is fake or fraudulent, then give the one-sentence diagnostic that separates the real from the fake
ac8e5cb1-7c2f-4b5e-b2bc-e3cd9bc29099.hook_template: [X]% of [hot sector] projects are just [fraudulent/superficial thing]. Here's the one test that separates the real ones from the cosplay.
ac8e5cb1-7c2f-4b5e-b2bc-e3cd9bc29099.psychological_trigger: Identity threat + contrarianism + utility — attacks an entire sector's credibility, forces insiders to either defend or agree, and promises actionable clarity as the reward for reading on
